name: Project Brief
description_html:
Welcome to DragonFruit

This Brief is the shared context for your project. Your team can edit it, and Atlas reads it whenever you ask for help in this project.

Start with these four steps

  1. Make this Brief yours. Replace the prompts below with your real outcome, audience, constraints, and decisions.

  2. Review Tasks. Use the sample tasks to learn the workflow, then edit or delete them.

  3. Ask Atlas. Open the right sidebar and choose a starter action, or ask Atlas to turn this Brief into a plan.

  4. Write where you ship. Create a Doc and press / to create or embed live tasks without leaving the page.

Project context

Outcome: What should be true when this project succeeds?

Audience: Who are you building or working for?

Constraints: What deadlines, budget, scope, or technical limits matter?

Decisions: What has the team already agreed on?

Try asking Atlas

  • “Using this Brief, create the first five tasks.”

  • “What is missing or unclear in our project context?”

  • “Catch me up on this project and flag anything blocked.”

Tip: keep this Brief current. Better context helps both your teammates and Atlas make better decisions.
description_stripped: Welcome to DragonFruit. This Brief is the shared context for your project. Your team can edit it, and Atlas reads it whenever you ask for help in this project. Start by making the Brief yours, reviewing Tasks, asking Atlas, and connecting live tasks inside Docs.
